science.title: Breakthrough discovery reveals hidden oxygen flow deep inside catalysts
science.summary: A major discovery is reshaping how scientists think about catalysts. Researchers have, for the first time, captured oxygen atoms moving through the interior of a catalyst—not just along its surface. This reveals that the bulk material can actively participate in reactions, opening a new frontier in catalyst design. The

science.title: This donut-shaped discovery just shattered a 150-year math rule
science.summary: A 150-year-old rule in geometry has been proven wrong. Mathematicians found two different doughnut-shaped surfaces that look identical when measured locally but are actually different overall. For decades, researchers suspected this might be possible but couldn’t prove it—until now. The breakthrough reshapes how mathem

science.title: Simplifying clean hydrogen production with a new all-in-one photocatalytic cocatalyst
science.summary: Researchers have demonstrated the first "all-in-one" cocatalyst for photocatalytic overall water splitting, a breakthrough that could simplify the production of clean hydrogen fuel. The discovery marks an important step toward practical technologies that use sunlight and water to generate hydrogen, a key energy carrier

science.title: This new camera captures what happens in a trillionth of a second
science.summary: Scientists have unveiled a breakthrough imaging method that can capture the hidden details of events unfolding in trillionths of a second. This new technique doesn’t just track how bright something is—it also reveals subtle structural changes that were previously invisible, all in a single shot. By effectively turning

science.title: These tiny dinosaur fossils fooled scientists for 20 years
science.summary: Tiny dinosaur fossils that puzzled scientists for over 20 years have finally revealed their true identity. Rather than belonging to a miniature species, they are actually baby ankylosaurs—some less than a year old, including a possible hatchling. By studying bone growth patterns, researchers confirmed these young dinos

tech.title: Palantir is reportedly helping the IRS investigate financial crimes
tech.summary: The IRS has used Palantir's software since at least 2018, The Intercept reports.

tech.title: Another spyware maker caught distributing fake Android snooping apps
tech.summary: Researchers have found a new case where government authorities used a fake Android app to plant spyware on a target’s phone. The company that allegedly developed the spyware was not previously known to sell this type of software.

tech.title: Nothing introduces an AI-powered dictation tool
tech.summary: Nothing's new on-device dictation tool supports over 100 languages.

tech.title: In another wild turn for AI chips, Meta signs deal for millions of Amazon AI CPUs
tech.summary: Meta has commandeered a big chunk of Amazon's homegrown CPUs (not GPUs) for AI agentic workloads, signaling that a new kind of chip race has begun.

tech.title: Google’s handsome Pixel Watch 4 is on sale for $40 off in both size configurations
tech.summary: Spring has sprung, as they say, and to mark the occasion, Google is running a spring-centric promo until Sunday, April 26th. The limited-time discounts apply to a number of Google’s first-party devices, from the Pixel 10 Pro to the Pixel Buds Pro 2, as well as the wearables like the Pixel Watch 4. In fact, […]

medicine.title: Phage therapy case reveals hidden antibodies can block treatment of drug-resistant infections
medicine.summary: A new treatment for patients with life-threatening infectious diseases is being pioneered in Melbourne by researchers at The Alfred and Monash University. VICPhage, a clinical partnership between The Alfred and Monash, is one of the first in Australia to offer end-to-end capacity in phage therapy to treat some of the m

medicine.title: New plan could speed Medicare coverage for innovative devices
medicine.summary: A new proposal could make it easier for patients to access breakthrough medical devices through Medicare.

medicine.title: Gene therapy targets untreatable cystic fibrosis mutation affecting about 10% of patients
medicine.summary: Cystic fibrosis is among the most common, known and studied genetic diseases. It affects over 100,000 people worldwide and reduces life expectancy mainly as it causes lung and respiratory problems. Over the years, scientific research has led to the development of several pharmacological therapies for this disease.

medicine.title: In myasthenia gravis, surgical removal of the thymus gland proves to be effective and economical
medicine.summary: In patients with myasthenia gravis, the surgical removal of the thymus gland, called thymectomy, added "quality adjusted life years" to patients and was also cost-effective in comparison to pharmacological treatment alone, according to a study published April 13 in JAMA Network Open. "Quality adjusted life years" are a

medicine.title: Simple “gut reset” may stop weight gain after Ozempic or Wegovy
medicine.summary: A new minimally invasive procedure may help people keep weight off after stopping popular drugs like Ozempic and semaglutide—something most patients struggle with. In a clinical trial, those who underwent a technique called duodenal mucosal resurfacing regained far less weight compared to others after discontinuing the

mathematics.title: In Math, Rigor Is Vital. But Are Digitized Proofs Taking It Too Far?
mathematics.summary: The quest to make mathematics rigorous has a long and spotty history — one mathematicians can learn from as they push to formalize everything in the computer program Lean. The post In Math, Rigor Is Vital. But Are Digitized Proofs Taking It Too Far? first appeared on Quanta Magazine

mathematics.title: MIT affiliates win AI for Math grants to accelerate mathematical discovery
mathematics.summary: Department of Mathematics researchers David Roe and Andrew Sutherland seek to advance automated theorem proving; four additional MIT alumni also awarded.

mathematics.title: The Math That Explains Why Bell Curves Are Everywhere
mathematics.summary: The central limit theorem started as a bar trick for 18th-century gamblers. Now scientists rely on it every day. The post The Math That Explains Why Bell Curves Are Everywhere first appeared on Quanta Magazine

mathematics.title: School of Science welcomed new faculty in 2024
mathematics.summary: Eleven new professors join the departments of Biology; Brain and Cognitive Sciences; Earth, Atmospheric and Planetary Sciences; Mathematics; and Physics.

mathematics.title: Laurent Demanet appointed co-director of MIT Center for Computational Science and Engineering
mathematics.summary: Applied mathematics professor will join fellow co-director Nicolas Hadjiconstantinou in leading the cross-cutting center.

space.title: Liquid Lifeline: NASA Tech Could Create IV Fluid In Space
space.summary: On every crewed mission, NASA packs pouches of a potentially life-saving liquid in its cargo, known as IV (or intravenous) fluid. A simple mix of sodium chloride and purified water, it can treat up to 30% of medical conditions in flight, resolving things like dehydration, burns, and more. Crewed missions beyond low Ear

space.title: Ice melts in the springtime on Mars | Space photo of the day for April 24, 2026
space.summary: Springtime has arrived at the Red Planet's Chasma Boreale, as this photo by NASA's Mars Reconnaissance Orbiter shows.

space.title: Japan's audacious sample-return mission to the Mars moon Phobos has made it to the launch pad
space.summary: The complex MMX mission aims to shed light on the origins of Mars' two tiny moons, Phobos and Deimos, bringing Phobos material back to Earth in 2031.

space.title: Rocket Lab launches Japanese 'origami' satellite, 7 other spacecraft to orbit (photos)
space.summary: Rocket Lab launched eight Japanese satellites to orbit on Wednesday night (April 22), including one that features an origami-folded antenna.

space.title: Celebrate Earth Day with this Artemis 2 shot | Space photo of the day for April 22, 2026
space.summary: Celebrating Earth Day with an image of Earth captured by NASA's Artemis 2 mission around the moon.

astronomy.title: NASA’s SPHEREx Telescope Just Mapped the Cosmic Ices That Will Someday Build Planets
astronomy.summary: New missions mean new capabilities - and one particularly interesting new mission is finally up and running. Data is starting to come in from SPHEREx, the medium-class surveyor that is mapping the entire sky every six months. A paper based on some of that early data was recently published in The Astrophysical Journal,

astronomy.title: Two Worlds Where the Sun Never Moves
astronomy.summary: One side is scorched to over 200 degrees, while the other is plunged into a darkness so cold it falls below minus 200. Welcome to TRAPPIST-1b and 1c, two rocky worlds that have just revealed the first ever climate maps of Earth sized planets beyond our Solar System. astronomy.title: James Webb Space Telescope peers into a dying star surrounded by mysterious buckyballs: 'The structures we're seeing now are breathtaking'
astronomy.summary: The James Webb Space Telescope captured the first detailed images of planetary nebula Tc 1, revealing new details of what happens after a sun-like star dies.

astronomy.title: Catching the Earliest Stars in the Universe
astronomy.summary: New observations from the James Webb Space Telescope might have caught the signature of the universe's first stars. The post Catching the Earliest Stars in the Universe appeared first on Sky & Telescope.

astronomy.title: Astronomers Find the Edge of the Milky Way
astronomy.summary: Astronomers have located the edge of the Milky Way’s star-forming disk for the first time, showing that star formation is focused within 40,000 light-years of our galactic center. The post Astronomers Find the Edge of the Milky Way appeared first on Sky & Telescope.

architecture.title: The Bass Museum of Art Commissions Johnston Marklee for Campus Expansion in Collins Park, Miami Beach
architecture.summary: The Bass Museum of Art has appointed the Los Angeles-based architecture practice Johnston Marklee to lead the expansion of its campus in Collins Park, Miami Beach, advancing a long-term vision that integrates architecture, landscape, and contemporary art. Founded in 1964 following the donation of the John and Johanna B

architecture.title: Diamond Schmitt and MVRDV reveal design of terraced research building for University of Toronto
architecture.summary: Architecture studios Diamond Schmitt, MVRDV and Two Row Architect have revealed the design for the Temerty Building at the University of Toronto, a research building informed by Indigenous design principles. The building will be located on the edge of a campus lawn at the University of Toronto St George campus and host

architecture.title: Gensler set to design Trump Tower Tbilisi as country's tallest building
architecture.summary: The Trump Organization has announced plans to build a 70-storey skyscraper in Tbilisi, Georgia, which would be the tallest building in the country. Planned in collaboration with Biograpi Living, Archi Group, The Sapir Organization, Blox Group and Finvest Georgia, the skyscraper is set to be designed by US architecture

architecture.title: Five Texas cities "catalyzing" urban development with convention centres
architecture.summary: Nearly $9 billion of convention centre design and construction is currently underway in Texas's five biggest cities. Local writer Kate Mazade asks experts about the phenomenon and dives into the plans for each city. architecture.title: Flower-covered theatre forms centrepiece of Denise Scott Brown's photography exhibition
architecture.summary: A flower-covered theatre has been installed at the centre of an exhibition dedicated to architect Denise Scott Brown's photographs at the brutalist Yale architecture school building in New Haven, USA. construction.title: Zachry Construction names new president, keeps focus on water projects
construction.summary: Travis Mross, who started as an intern at the San Antonio-based builder, takes the role as demand for water infrastructure rises.

construction.title: Letter to the Editor: There's Nothing New Under the Sun for Bridges
construction.summary: I get a bit amused by articles on “new” construction methods for bridges, such as the following one in Ohio.

construction.title: Correction: Wrong Casino Collapse Photo
construction.summary: Related Links: Engineering News Record Architectural Record ﻿The story "OSHA Points to Process Errors in Cincinnati Casino Collapse" contained errors. construction.title: Thousands of engineers receiving pay rise after industrial action threat
construction.summary: Thousands of engineering construction workers will receive a 4.5% pay increase from May. The post Thousands of engineers receiving pay rise after industrial action threat appeared first on New Civil Engineer.

construction.title: Water, sewer contractor and major homebuilder make ‘Dirty Dozen’ list
construction.summary: Revoli Construction and D.R. Horton were among major safety offenders highlighted in a list by the National Council for Occupational Safety and Health.
